### Session 209afff1-837b-478c-af92-0024322f9f93 - 2026-07-13T04:36:20.260Z

- User questioned why manual routing saved 1 hour 12 minutes compared to paid OptimoRoute optimization — something felt wrong.
- Claude Code investigated and identified two root causes: (1) OptimoRoute's route was constrained by hidden order priorities (C/H/M tiers) that forced geographic inefficiency, not solver failure; (2) Jobber's board had drifted from Optimo's plan (two stops in different positions after write-back).
- Found the priority encoding in `push-week.mjs:187` — new bookings were auto-tagged priority C, forcing the optimizer to serve them early in the day regardless of geography.
- Quantified the impact: user's hand-routed sequence = 106 km stop-to-stop; Optimo's constrained plan = 133 km (20% longer route from serving priorities rather than pure geography).
- Claude Code proposed four fixes: (1) remove priority-C encoding for new bookings, use actual time windows instead; (2) reset stray H priorities to M in Optimo; (3) add Stella Sun's real window (8:55–12:00 PM) to Optimo; (4) add pre-finalization drift check between Optimo and Jobber boards.
- Fixes identified as quick (one-line script edit + batch Optimo update) with drift check to fold into queued WF-1/WF-2 rebuild.

### Session 209afff1-837b-478c-af92-0024322f9f93 - 2026-07-13T04:44:54.717Z

- User clarified route-optimization philosophy: all orders must have equal priority to achieve shortest possible route; time-window promises come FROM the Optimo plan, not fed in as constraints (except real customer-promised windows).
- Claude Code confirmed all changes live: four route-push scripts now send every order at uniform priority M (was tagging new bookings as priority C, causing OptimoRoute to front-load them and distort daily sequences).
- Live account swept 07-13–07-26 (508 stops): found 70 elevated-priority orders (13 C from script, 57 H hand-set in Optimo UI); all reset to M; canary-tested safety (no unscheduling); confirmed zero remain.
- Stella Sun's 8:55 AM–12:00 PM promise now encoded as real time window on her Optimo order, protecting it even if planning re-runs.
- Decision point identified: rest of planned week (07-14–07-26) was sequenced under old warped priorities and could be re-optimized, but requires promised-window list first to avoid moving any already-committed times.
